title: Church & Dwight (CHD) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Church & Dwight (CHD) compiled entirely by Gemini.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 5% growth rate is applied, reflecting Church & Dwight's consistent, single-digit organic growth supplemented by strategic bolt-on acquisitions in the consumer staples space.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.0% discount rate reflects the highly defensive nature of the company's product portfolio and its consistent cash flow generation, mitigating risk.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term inflation targets and population growth, acknowledging the mature nature of the consumer goods industry.

thesisParagraphs: Church & Dwight's true value lies in its portfolio of essential consumer brands. This portfolio generates highly predictable cash flows, as consumer purchasing behavior for these staples remains resilient regardless of broader economic conditions.

The company's proven strategy of acquiring and integrating niche brands provides a reliable engine for long-term, steady growth. Its defensive posture makes it an attractive core holding in any portfolio.
verdictFooter: Based on recent financial disclosures. Analysis compiled by Gemini.

faq.question: Why a 5% growth rate for Church & Dwight?
faq.answer: This rate balances the inherently slower growth of mature consumer staples with the company's successful history of strategic acquisitions.

faq.question: How do private label brands impact valuation?
faq.answer: Private labels present an ongoing threat, but Church & Dwight's strong brand loyalty and competitive pricing mitigate this risk.

faq.question: Why use an 8% discount rate?
faq.answer: The lower discount rate reflects the reduced volatility and lower risk associated with providing essential, everyday consumer products.
